spec_match.rationale: Page runs script and renders dynamic UI, but the visible API surface (window., document.querySelector, ai.languageModel) only partially mirrors what the feature summary calls out — the headline behaviour isn't unambiguously demonstrated.

interactive.rationale: Some controls present but the interactive surface is thin (2 buttons / 0 inputs / 0 selects). Hard to call it hands-on.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept lede matches the feature-index promise on key terms (case, detection, edge, fallback, graceful, invalid, reuse, session, temperature, tests, topk).

edge_cases.rationale: Some failure / fallback handling shown (15 hits), but the demo skews toward the happy path and doesn't surface most edge cases the spec calls out.

references.rationale: Page includes the required chromestatus.com/feature/6325545693478912 link in a references section.

design_system.rationale: 7 raw hex colour(s) still in the style block. The design system requires CSS variables (`--bg-ivory`, `--text-charcoal` etc.)

openQuestions.title: Tighten the link between the demo and the spec's headline behaviour
openQuestions.detail: Confirm the on-screen interaction maps to a concrete API call from the feature summary on chromestatus: "Adds sampling parameters to the Prompt API. These control how tokens are sampled from the model, giving developers control over the "creativeness" or "randomness" of the output. Additionally, it adds ". If the call is being shimmed or simulated, surface that fact in the page and add a real invocation behind a feature-detect.

openQuestions.title: Expand the interactive surface beyond one or two buttons
openQuestions.detail: Add controls that vary the parameters the spec exposes — toggles, ranges, selects — so the user can drive the demo through several distinct states instead of a single one-shot trigger.

openQuestions.title: Demo at least one explicit failure or edge case
openQuestions.detail: Spec / chromestatus motivation mentions failure modes worth showing — permission denial, missing-feature, empty input, malformed payload. Add a control or scripted scenario that walks the user through one and shows the resulting error or fallback.

openQuestions.title: Swap raw hex colours for CSS variables
openQuestions.detail: Find the remaining hex literals in the inline `<style>` and replace each with the matching variable from `public/styles.css`. Re-check WCAG AA on any new pair you create.

openQuestions.title: Manually verify against the spec text
openQuestions.detail: Reviewer-v1-subagent based the spec_match verdict on the chromestatus summary ("Adds sampling parameters to the Prompt API. These control how tokens are sampled from the model, giving developers control over the "creativeness" or "randomness" of the output. Additionally, it adds attributes to the LanguageModel instance") because reading the full spec for every concept was out of budget. A human or follow-up agent should diff the demo's actual behaviour against https://webmachinelearning.github.io/prompt-api to catch anything chromestatus glossed over.

summary: Concept ships ~12873 chars of script across 2 button(s) and 0 other control(s). hits 2 pass / 4 partial / 0 fail across the six rubric criteria.
